label: Boutique Warm
description: Smaller curated storefronts with lifestyle framing and richer product storytelling.
signatureMotif: editorial storefront framing, warm surfaces, and tactile merchandising
promptHints: Lead with merchandising and product mood before dense catalog controls.
Product storytelling should feel curated rather than catalog-first.

layouts: Use a split hero with editorial lifestyle photography on one side and a tight product story card with price and CTA on the other.
Open collection pages with a featured product strip above the grid, then transition into a calm 3-column catalog with generous gutters.
Build product detail pages as a two-column composition with large imagery, stacked purchase controls, and a warm storytelling block below.
Use gift-guide sections as staggered editorial cards, mixing product cutouts, short narrative captions, and occasional highlighted picks.
Frame trust and brand story as alternating image-and-copy bands, ending with a compact newsletter or in-store pickup callout.
motifs: Warm cream backgrounds, soft white product cards, and earthy accent buttons create a tactile boutique storefront at first glance.
Large serif headlines paired with restrained sans body text give product storytelling an editorial magazine-shop feel.
Use 1rem rounded corners, thin warm-gray borders, and very soft shadows so merchandise feels curated instead of tech-polished.
Lifestyle photography should feel natural and softly lit, with product cutouts and detail crops used as supporting merchandising moments.
antiPatterns: Avoid dense marketplace-style filter rails dominating the first screen; this variant should introduce mood and curation before control-heavy catalog UI.
Never use cold monochrome minimalism or stark black-and-white contrast; the storefront should feel warm, giftable, and human.
Avoid loud promo badges, flashing sale strips, or aggressive conversion banners that undermine the calm boutique presentation.
Do not make product grids feel warehouse-like with tiny cards and cramped spacing; items need breathing room and storytelling context.
